channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The CTO Playbook for AI Agent Data Analysis on a Budget
item.description: So here's what happened: the CTO Playbook for AI Agent Data Analysis on a Budget Six months ago my engineering team was burning roughly $14,000 a month on a single AI agent data pipeline. The...

item.title: AMD ATOM + ATOMesh: Prefill/decode Disaggregation on ROCm
item.description: What: AMD shipped ATOM + ATOMesh, a ROCm-native LLM serving stack whose headline trick is prefill/decode disaggregation — splitting the two phases of inference onto separate pools of GPUs instead...

item.title: 👉 "React Native New Architecture Migration: What Broke and How We Fixed It"
item.description: Recently, I migrated a production React Native application from the Legacy Architecture to the New Architecture (Fabric + TurboModules + JSI). What looked like a simple configuration change:...

item.title: Diagnosing a slow Rails page, layer by layer
item.description: "This page feels slow" is a vague bug report. Before changing any code, it helps to have a fixed way to locate where the time goes. A Rails request passes through a few predictable layers, and...

item.title: Anti-Bot Evasion 2026: Why Your TLS Handshake Is Getting You Flagged (And How to Fix It)
item.description: Why Your Browser Version Could Be Exposing Your Scraper Before the First Request Modern anti-bot systems no longer rely solely on HTTP headers, JavaScript fingerprints, or IP reputation....

item.title: Blackbox Monitoring vs Internal Metrics - The Gap Between "Healthy" and "Working"
item.description: You've probably had this incident. Dashboards are all green. CPU is fine. Memory looks good. Pods aren't restarting. Databases are healthy. But customers can't log in, or payments won't process,...

item.title: Your Body, Their Data
item.description: Every morning, roughly 62 million Americans strap on a fitness tracker, open a wellness app, or tap through a mood journal before their first cup of coffee. They log sleep scores, heart rate...

item.title: Sony’s Xperia 1 VIII is still a phone for the fans
item.description: The Xperia 1 VIII marks an attempt at a step change for Sony's flagship phone line. Not only has it had an aesthetic overhaul, but Sony has also revamped the camera system, dropping the continuous...

item.title: Prompt injection and LLM security for SaaS
item.description: Originally published on 475 Cumulus Prompt injection and LLM security for SaaS A practical security guide for multi-tenant products — why system prompts are not enough, where attacks actually...

item.title: Kubernetes and Cloud Native Associate (KCNA) Study Notes
item.description: I sat the Kubernetes and Cloud Native Associate (KCNA) and passed. KCNA is the entry-level CNCF certification - it covers Kubernetes fundamentals and the wider cloud native landscape at a...

item.title: Stop Copying Your .pre-commit-config.yaml
item.description: Stop Copy-Pasting: Introducing pc-init We’ve all been there: you’re starting a new project, you’ve got your repo initialized, and now comes the tedious part—setting up the quality gates. You know...

item.title: 28 Tips to Take Your ChatGPT Prompts to the Next Level
item.description: Sure, anyone can use OpenAI’s chatbot. But with smart engineering, you can get way more interesting results.

item.title: Signal’s Meredith Whittaker wants you to remember that AI chatbots ‘are not your friends’
item.description: "These are not your friends. These are not conscious beings. These are not sentient interlocutors.”

item.title: In the Weights is your new AI-centric vanity search
item.description: So ... what's your In the Weights score?

item.title: The Atlantic created a searchable database of the music used to train AI
item.description: Atlantic reporter Alex Reisner recently uncovered four datasets of music being used to train AI models and made them fully searchable for the public. Two of the sets are absolutely enormous at 12...

item.title: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for rival Anthropic
item.description: Jumper isn't the only big name leaving Google DeepMind.

item.title: Musician and YouTuber Hainbach on ‘Breath of the Wild’ and Swiss Army Knives
item.description: Stefan Paul Goetsch, better known as Hainbach, is a German experimental composer, artist, and YouTuber who is perhaps most famous for making music with laboratory equipment and scientific...

item.title: Moves of the Diamond Hand is an unfinished, irresistibly weird dice-based RPG
item.description: From its opening minutes, Moves of the Diamond Hand is upfront about what it offers: You're going to have a lot of strange conversations, and you're going to roll a lot of dice. Get on board with...

item.title: Toy Story has the right take on tech
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 133, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, happy belated Juneteenth, and also you can read all the old...

item.title: Siri AI Hands On: A Smart, Helpful Assistant
item.description: The new Siri AI is conversational, omnipresent, and actually helpful.

item.title: Hackers Exploit Gravity SMTP WordPress Plugin Bug to Expose API Keys
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting a recently patched security flaw impacting Gravity SMTP, a WordPress plugin that's installed on about 100,000 sites. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-4020 (CVSS...

item.title: SwitchBot’s Standing Circulator Fan is worth fighting for
item.description: I can't remember the last time I got excited about a fan. Normally, I just buy whatever Vornado or Dreo model fits my budget, but that was before I started testing the battery-powered Standing...

item.title: From PGP to Mythos: a brief history of export controls that didn’t stop anyone
item.description: For the last 30 years, stopping the flow of cybersecurity-related software has proven to be ineffective. It's unclear why it would work now with Anthropic’s cybersecurity model Mythos.

item.title: Nothing cancels this year’s CMF phone due to RAM prices
item.description: Nothing's next budget phone is the latest victim of RAMageddon. As 9to5Google reports, Nothing co-founder Akis Evangelidis announced in a post on X that a follow-up to the CMF Phone 2 Pro won't be...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Victims of Unregulated Squid Fishing
item.description: Dolphins, sharks, turtles, and human workers are all victims of unregulated squid fishing fleets. Another news article. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security...

item.title: NASA selects Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for a 2028 mission to Mars
item.description: Relativity Space, the rocket company led by former Google executive Eric Schmidt, was picked to launch NASA's Aeolus payload to Mars in 2028, as reported earlier by TechCrunch. Under a new...

item.title: Unpatchable 'usbliter8' Exploit Breaks Apple A12 and A13 SecureROM Boot Chain
item.description: Security researchers at Paradigm Shift have published a working exploit, dubbed usbliter8, that achieves arbitrary code execution inside the SecureROM of Apple's A12 and A13 chips. That code is...

item.title: The Gentlemen RaaS Uses GentleKiller EDR Framework Targeting 400 Security Processes
item.description: The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation is actively developing and maintaining a suite of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers that it hands out to affiliates for impairing...

item.title: Hue’s wired wall modules bring non-smart lights into its ecosystem
item.description: Smart lighting company Philips Hue has launched its first wired wall modules. Installed behind existing wall switches, the new devices bring non-smart lights into the Hue ecosystem for the first...

item.title: Is the US government’s Anthropic ban accidentally helping the brand?
item.description: Just as last week was ending, the US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly found a...
